data.title: Binance will delist SLERF and CHZ perpetual contracts and adjust leverage
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Futures will delist the USDⓈ-MSLERFUSDT perpetual contract at 09:00 (UTC) on October 21, 2025, and the COIN-MCHZUSD perpetual contract at 10:00 (UTC) on the same day. Automatic liquidation and settlement will be implemented at these times. Additionally, Binance will update the leverage and margin tiers for the SLERFUSDT contract at 06:30 (UTC) on October 20, 2025. Users are advised to manage their positions in advance. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: OpenSea CEO: Plans to launch SEA token in the first quarter of 2026
data.description: OpenSea CEO Devin Finzer announced plans to launch the SEA token in the first quarter of 2026. 50% of the total SEA supply will be allocated to the community, with half allocated for initial claims. Participants in the OpenSea rewards program and OG users will be given priority. The launch of SEA will provide users with more ways to utilize the token, including staking, allowing them to stake SEA against their favorite tokens and collectibles. Furthermore, 50% of SEA revenue will be used to purchase SEA at launch, ensuring the token's value and the health of the ecosystem.

data.title: Falcon Finance will launch its first NFT series "Perryverse" in October
data.description: According to Falcon Finance, its first NFT series, "Perryverse," will begin whitelist minting on October 22nd, with public minting opening on October 23rd. Users must stake 1,000 $FF tokens to qualify for the whitelist draw. The whitelist minting price is 1,000 FF, while the public minting price is 2,000 FF, with a total supply of 3,500. The NFTs are themed around eggs, each with a unique appearance and hidden attributes. Their rarity will influence the second season's "Falcon Miles" bonus. Officials have confirmed that the whitelist snapshot will be October 20th.

data.title: Market Caution Amid Deleveraging and Credit Concerns, Says Tom Lee
data.description:
According to PANews, Tom Lee, co-founder of Fundstrat and chairman of BitMine, analyzed the current cautious state of the market on the X platform. He attributed this to several factors, including significant deleveraging in the crypto market last week, concerns over a 'cockroach crisis' in private credit, and the typical 'market explosion' seen in October. The term 'cockroach crisis' was recently used by JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon to describe potential risks in the U.S. credit market, suggesting that the discovery of one issue often indicates more underlying problems. This sector, valued at $3 trillion, is characterized by weak regulation and insufficient transparency.

Despite these concerns, Lee highlighted that negative market sentiment could lead to contrarian benefits. He noted that many fund managers are underperforming compared to industry benchmarks, which may prompt them to accelerate their pursuit of investment returns before the year's end. Consequently, Lee advised investors to 'Buy The Dip,' suggesting that current market conditions could present favorable buying opportunities.

data.title: Bitcoin Cycle Score Turns Negative With Trend Below $106,780 – When Will The Correction End?
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) continues to lose momentum, as the flagship cryptocurrency fell to $103,528 earlier today amid an increasingly uncertain global macroeconomic outlook. Fresh data from Binance suggests that BTC is currently undergoing a critical transition phase within its price cycle. Bitcoin Fall Continues – When Will Bloodbath End? According to a CryptoQuant QuickTake post by contributor Arab Chain, Bitcoin is currently undergoing an important transition phase within its market cycle. The Bitcoin Cycle Phase Score recently entered negative territory, in tandem with a decline in BTC’s price from $124,000 to around $107,000 within 24 hours
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-cycle-score-negative-trend-106780-correction/

data.title: U.S. Ethereum Spot ETF Sees Significant Outflow
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Farside Investors indicates a substantial net outflow of $232 million from U.S. Ethereum spot ETFs. The breakdown of the outflows is as follows: BlackRock's ETHA experienced a decrease of $146.1 million, Fidelity's FETH saw a reduction of $30.6 million, Bitwise's ETHW recorded a $20.6 million outflow, 21 Shares' ETHV had a $4.2 million decrease, Grayscale's ETHE faced a $26.1 million outflow, and Grayscale Mini ETH saw a reduction of $4.7 million. This movement reflects a significant shift in investor sentiment towards Ethereum ETFs in the U.S. market.

data.title: Hackers panic-sold 7,816 ETH again, with cumulative losses exceeding $13.4 million
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, six wallets associated with the hacker panic-sold 7,816 ETH (worth approximately $29.14 million) at an average price of $3,728 during the market downturn, resulting in a further loss of approximately $3.37 million. Statistics show that these addresses have recently incurred cumulative losses exceeding $13.4 million due to buying high and selling low. Previously, during the previous market crash, the hacker also sold 8,638 ETH at an average price of $3,764, resulting in a loss of approximately $5.5 million.

data.title: Tether Gold's market capitalization exceeds $2 billion, hitting a record high
data.description: Coingecko data shows that the market capitalization of Tether's gold-backed stablecoin, Tether Gold (XAUT), has surpassed $2 billion, reaching $2,219,853,037, a new all-time high, with a circulating supply of 522,089 coins. Furthermore, the total market capitalization of the tokenized gold sector has surpassed $3.95 billion, reaching $3,950,937,387, also a new high, with a 24-hour increase of 15.4%.

data.title: "Big Brother Maji" adds another ETH long position and adds 220,000 USDC to Hyperliquid
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Huang Licheng, known as "Big Brother," received 220,000 USDC from QCP Capital and transferred it to the Hyperliquid platform, where he continued to increase his long ETH position. His current position is approximately 1,439 ETH, valued at approximately $5.5 million, with a liquidation price of $3,734. He has previously experienced multiple margin calls during market fluctuations.

data.title: Dogecoin (DOGE) Falls 10% to $0.17 as Whales Dump $74M Despite Nasdaq Merger Hype
data.description:
In the past 24 hours, Dogecoin (DOGE)’s price slipped another 10% to $0. 17, extending a weekly drop of more than 27% as on-chain data showed whales unloading roughly 360 million DOGE ($74 million). Related Reading: Why The Dogecoin Price Could Still Hit A 600% Rally To Send It Above $1. 5 The selloff arrived despite upbeat headlines around House of Doge’s plan to merge with a Nasdaq-listed company and Thumzup’s exploration of DOGE payouts for creators
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/dogecoin/dogecoin-doge-falls-10-to-0-17-as-whales-dump-74m-despite-nasdaq-merger-hype/

data.title: Smart money Hanmancheol's real-time short position on COAI went bankrupt, resulting in a loss of over $1.37 million in three days.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, Binance's real-time smart money account, Hanmancheol, continuously shorted the COAI token over three consecutive days, paying a total of $774,000 in funding fees, with an average entry price of $10.79 and an average liquidation price of $18.97, resulting in a loss of $1.378 million. The account had previously made profits on six futures contracts this month, but this still failed to offset the losses from the liquidation.

data.title: A suspected Blockchain Capital address deposited 28 million SKY tokens to CEXs such as Binance.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, about 35 minutes ago, an address marked as suspected Blockchain Capital deposited a total of 28 million SKY to three exchanges: Binance, OKX, and Bybit. Among them, 20 million SKY (worth $1.18 million) was transferred to Binance, 4 million SKY (worth $236,060) was transferred to OKX, and 4 million SKY (worth $236,060) was transferred to Bybit.

data.title: Hackers Incur Losses Amid Ethereum Market Downturn
data.description: According to PANews, several hackers have reportedly sold off 7,816 ETH, valued at $29.14 million, during a market decline, resulting in a loss of $3.37 million. The sale occurred at a price of $3,728 per ETH. Recently, six hacker wallets have collectively lost over $13.4 million due to buying high and selling low in the Ethereum market.

data.title: Crypto stocks closed mixed at the close of the US stock market, with COIN up 1.75%
data.description: According to data from msx.com, US stocks closed up 0.52% on Friday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average, the S&P 500 up 0.53%, and the Nasdaq up 0.52%. Crypto stocks showed mixed performance, with Coinbase (COIN) leading the gains with a 1.75% increase, Strategy (MSTR) up 2.12%, and BTCS (BTCS) up 3.34%. New SOL treasury stock Helius (HSDT) saw the largest declines, falling 23.24%, while ALT5 Sigma (ALTS) fell 11.19%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, including US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.
